title: Charter to Participate in UBS Global Media and Communications Conference
text: STAMFORD, Conn. , Nov. 28, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) (along with its subsidiaries, "Charter") today announced that Jessica Fischer, Chief Financial Officer, will participate in the UBS Global Media and Communications Conference in New York, New York on Tuesday, December 5, 2023.

title: Charter Communications to pay $25 mln penalty for unauthorized stock buybacks, SEC says
text: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said on Tuesday that Charter Communications (CHTR.O) will pay a $25 million penalty for unauthorized stock buybacks.

title: Charter Communications to Pay $25 Million Penalty for Unauthorized Stock Buybacks
text: Washington, D.C.--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2023) - The Securities and Exchange Commission today announced settled charges against Charter Communications Inc. for violating internal accounting controls requirements when it engaged in stock buybacks not authorized by its board of directors.

title: 10 media stocks expected by Wall street analysts to rise as much as 60% in 2024
text: The Communications Services sector of the S&P 500 is a mixed bag of companies growing revenue quickly and others with business models that are mature or undergoing transformation. Some of the latter have been crushed this year, but that could underline opportunities for 2024.

title: Charter Closes $2.0 Billion Senior Secured Notes
text: STAMFORD, Conn. , Nov. 10, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) (along with its subsidiaries, "Charter") today announced that its subsidiaries, Charter Communications Operating, LLC and Charter Communications Operating Capital Corp. (collectively, the "Issuers"), have closed their offering of $2.0 billion in aggregate principal amount of notes consisting of the following securities: $1.1 billion in aggregate principal amount of Senior Secured Notes due 2026 (the "2026 Notes").

title: 8 Stocks That Hit the Trifecta: Growth, Value, and Momentum
text: Upstream oil and gas producers, Charter Communications, and Jabil pass a Barron's stock screen.

title: Charter Prices $2.0 Billion Senior Secured Notes
text: STAMFORD, Conn. , Nov. 7, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) (along with its subsidiaries, "Charter") today announced that its subsidiaries, Charter Communications Operating, LLC and Charter Communications Operating Capital Corp. (collectively, the "Issuers"), have priced $2.0 billion in aggregate principal amount of notes consisting of the following securities: $1.1 billion in aggregate principal amount of Senior Secured Notes due 2026 (the "2026 Notes").

title: Charter Offers Senior Secured Notes
text: STAMFORD, Conn. , Nov. 7, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) (along with its subsidiaries, "Charter") today announced that its subsidiaries, Charter Communications Operating, LLC and Charter Communications Operating Capital Corp. (collectively, the "Issuers"), intend to offer senior secured fixed rate notes (the "Notes").

title: Charter to Participate in Liberty Broadband Corporation's Annual Investor Meeting
text: STAMFORD, Conn. , Nov. 6, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) (along with its subsidiaries, "Charter") today announced that Christopher Winfrey, President and Chief Executive Officer, will participate in Liberty Broadband Corporation's (NASDAQ: LBRDA, LBRDK, LBRDP) annual Investor Meeting in New York, New York on Thursday, November 9, 2023.

title: Michael Burry's third-biggest stock position soars 18% in a day
text: Earlier this year, the investing world was taken aback when legendary investor Michael Burry, known for his lucrative ‘Big Short' against the housing market in 2008, made another bearish bet, this time targeting US stocks.

title: “Hard-Bundle” Carriage Deals Resembling The Recent Charter-Disney Pact Appear To Be In Paramount's U.S. Future, CEO Bob Bakish Says
text: Paramount CEO Bob Bakish has long touted “hard bundles,” the hybrid carriage agreements found in Europe and other international markets that weave streaming into conventional pay-TV packages.
